description: A hierarchical random-effect offset for a structural covariate (shift, program) on a Golden outcome — Achse 2 of the two-axis Golden model. Partial-pooling: sparse levels shrink toward the population mean, so 'Schicht 2 +1.2% aber innerhalb der Streuung — keine Schicht ist besser' falls out naturally (statistically honest answers, §5). Decision-rule: deliberate setpoints are NOT here (they are SetupModes); exploratory/sparse factors (best hour, operator) are NOT here either (at-query-time views in Layer C). threshold_activated gates promotion to this Stufe-2 conditioning granularity (Konzept Open Question §27.4).

attributes.name: effect_id
attributes.description: Unique effect id (golden_id + dimension + covariate + level)

attributes.name: dimension
attributes.description: Which outcome dimension this effect modifies

attributes.name: offset
attributes.description: Shrunk offset vs the population (base Golden) mean

attributes.name: ci_low
attributes.description: Lower credible-interval bound of the offset

attributes.name: ci_high
attributes.description: Upper credible-interval bound of the offset

attributes.name: pooled
attributes.description: True while fully shrunk (too few obs to separate from the population)

attributes.name: significant
attributes.description: Whether the offset's CI excludes zero — drives statistically honest answers (no pseudo-ranking)

attributes.name: threshold_activated
attributes.description: Whether this covariate has crossed the activation threshold for Stufe-2 conditioning
